---
title: "Onboarding practice submissions"
description: "A synthetic area for one small participant repository-practice contribution."
---

This area supports the [onboarding pilot](../pilot/index.qmd). A practice submission lets a participant exercise the documented Issue, branch, commit, push, pull-request, and review workflow with a deliberately small Quarto file.

## File naming

Copy `pilot/submission-template.qmd` to `practice/submissions/<assigned-alias>.qmd`. Replace the placeholder with the assigned lowercase synthetic alias, using only letters, numbers, and hyphens. Do not overwrite another submission or add a file outside `practice/submissions/`.

## Synthetic content only

Every submission must have a nonempty title and `synthetic: true`. It may contain only the assigned fictional alias or public GitHub username, a fictional role, one exercise sentence, the onboarding checklist, and an optional one-sentence workflow reflection. The [submission template](../pilot/submission-template.qmd) lists prohibited information.

Automated validation checks front matter only. A steward must still review the diff for unsafe or unrelated content.

## Contribution workflow

Work from a manually created practice Issue and follow the [participant exercise](../pilot/participant-exercise.qmd). Review every changed line, validate the submission, open a pull request against `main`, link the Issue with the instructed closing keyword, and request steward review. Nothing on this page creates an Issue, branch, or pull request.

## Example, not a participant record

The [example participant submission](submissions/example-participant.qmd) is entirely fictional and demonstrates structure only. It is not evidence that a real person completed the pilot. Do not add completed observation records to this directory.
